instruction: Can you provide details on CVE-2023-42866?
output:
CVE-2023-42866 is a high-severity vulnerability that could allow arbitrary code execution when processing web content due to inadequate memory handling. This vulnerability impacts Apple software, including macOS prior to Ventura 13.5, iOS and iPadOS prior to 16.6, tvOS prior to 16.6, Safari prior to 16.6, and watchOS prior to 9.6.

The vulnerability is categorized under the MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1203. It highlights how exploitation could enable malicious actors to execute arbitrary code on affected systems, potentially compromising system integrity.

Affected users should update their software to the latest versions as recommended. Mitigation techniques such as M1050 (improve process isolation), M1051 (implement code integrity policy), and M1048 (implement application layer filtering) can further secure systems against similar threats.
